member.office_address: 426 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3010

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. LaMonica McIver Programmatic/Language Request Form for FY27
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: This form is for the submission of programmatic and language requests for FY27 only. Community Project Funding requests should be submitted at this link. Please complete one form per request, providing as much information as possible. The submission deadline for Programmatic and Language requests is Monday, March 9. If you need assistance in completing this form, please contact Layla Brooks at layla.brooks@mail.house.gov or call our office at (202) 225-3436. Official guidance from the House Committee on Appropriations is available at this link.

fields.label: By submitting this form, you acknowledge that requests must be evaluated on merit, comply with House rules, and that you have read the relevant materials published by the House Appropriations Committee.
fields.rationale: This is a standard acknowledgement of committee rules required for all submissions.

fields.label: What kind of request are you submitting?
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for the Science Mission Directorate, which is categorized as a programmatic request.

fields.label: Under what appropriations bill or subcommittee does this request fall?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy, Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding is governed by the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Federal Agency
fields.rationale: Standard agency name for this request as per reference values.

fields.label: Program Name
fields.rationale: Standard program name for this request as per reference values.

fields.label: Requested report language
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request; context indicates no report language is being requested at this time.

fields.label: Are you making this request to other congressional offices?
fields.rationale: This request is being submitted to multiple offices.

fields.label: Briefly justify this request.
fields.rationale: This field appears to be tied to the report language request section, which does not apply to this programmatic request.

fields.label: Why is inclusion of this language important to New Jersey's 10th Congressional District? Please provide as much substantive justification as possible.
fields.rationale: This field appears to be tied to the report language request section, which does not apply to this programmatic request.

fields.label: Federal Agency
fields.rationale: Standard agency name for this request as per reference values.

fields.label: Program/Account Name
fields.rationale: Standard program/account name for this request as per reference values.

fields.label: Requested funding level, FY27
fields.rationale: This is the specific FY2027 funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate provided in the reference values.

fields.label: President's Budget Request (if applicable), FY27
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet according to the reference data.

fields.label: Enacted funding level, FY26
fields.help_text: Enter the enacted dollar amount from the prior fiscal year.
fields.rationale: This is the FY2026 enacted level specified in the reference values and the field's pre-filled annotation.

fields.label: Are you making this request to other congressional offices?
fields.rationale: This request is being submitted to multiple offices.

fields.label: Briefly justify this funding request.
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion request supports peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities and ensures U.S. leadership in the face of rapid Chinese space science expansion.
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns $3 to the economy, supporting high-tech manufacturing and the national STEM workforce pipeline.
* Restores funding stability following FY2026 disruptions, protecting essential civil servant and contractor capabilities necessary for major missions.
* Maintains the United States' unique position as the only nation to reach every major body in the solar system, a feat critical to national prestige and soft power.
fields.rationale: Justification uses the $9B ask amount and connects it to primary advocacy talking points regarding U.S. leadership, economic ROI, and workforce stability.

fields.label: Why is congressional authorization of this funding important to New Jersey's 10th Congressional District? Please provide as much substantive justification for this funding level as possible.
fields.draft_value:
* Direct local impact of $8,214,298 in NASA Science obligations within NJ-10 between FY2023 and FY2025.
* Supports the New Jersey Institute of Technology with over $10M in grants for critical research into solar dynamics and space weather forecasting.
* Sustains specialized local industry, including advanced technical component contracts with Newark-based Unique Wire Weaving Co Inc.
* Strengthens the local STEM talent pipeline at NJIT, preparing the Newark-area workforce for leadership in the high-growth aerospace and technology sectors.
fields.rationale: Cites specific NASA spending data for NJ-10, including the $8.2M obligation total, NJIT grant values, and Unique Wire Weaving Co Inc as a local contractor.

fields.label: Was this account subject to DOGE or other Trump Administration cuts since January 2025? If so, please describe.
fields.draft_value:
* FY2026 President's Budget Request proposed a $3.342 billion (46%) reduction to the NASA Science Mission Directorate compared to FY2025 enacted levels.
* Proposed cuts targeted nearly all scientific divisions, threatening thousands of workforce positions across civil service and industry partners.
* Bipartisan majorities in Congress rejected these cuts in FY2026 (H.R. 6938) to restore funding to $7.250 billion.
* Sustained investment is required to recover from the workforce instability and project delays caused by these proposed reductions.
fields.rationale: Detailed the massive FY2026 proposed cut ($3.908B request vs $7.334B FY25 enacted) and subsequent congressional restoration as provided in the SMD topline funding history.

fields.label: Please attach letters of support here.
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

fields.label: Please include any other notes for NJ-10 staff here.
fields.draft_value: NASA Science has invested over $8.2 million in NJ-10 since FY23, primarily supporting critical space weather and solar research at the New Jersey Institute of Technology in Newark. These investments strengthen our local STEM workforce and help the U.S. maintain its leadership in space science against growing international competition.
fields.rationale: This response connects the funding request to specific local impacts in NJ-10, highlighting the over $8 million in spending and the specific work at NJIT in Newark.

fields.label: I confirm that the answers given here are complete and correct to the best of my knowledge and am ready to submit.
fields.rationale: Required confirmation to proceed with the form submission.

top_grants.description: Our proposed effort is well aligned with the first of the three hso-connect program objectives: investigate the structure and dynamics of phenomena and/or mechanisms responsible for processes at the sun and inner heliosphere.

recipient_location.address_line1: 323 DR MARTIN LUTHER KING JR BLVD

top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing solar dynamics affecting the inner solar system

top_grants.description: The topic of the proposed work is ionospheric scintillation in the polar-cap ionosphere  more specifically  scintillation measured in the high frequency (hf 3-30 mhz) portion of the radio spectrum. The overarching objective of the is to advance our understanding of ionospheric scintillation in the northern polar-cap ionosphere through the analysis of radio science data from an hf receiver in low Earth orbit  and modeling studies. The proposed work is guided by three science questions: do diffractive effects contribute significantly to hf scintillation in the polar cap what geophysical phenomena are responsible for hf scintillation and  what is the relationship between hf scintillation and radar scattering in the polar-cap ionosphere? To accomplish this  we will analyze 7 years of spaceborne hf receiver measurements (OVER 350) of well-known and characterized hf radar systems located in the high-latitude region for signatures of ionospheric scintillation. Ionospheric and hf ray trace modeling will be used to synthesize magnetoionic  instrumental  and other expected contributors of fluctuations in amplitude and phase measured by the receive  to help identify scintillation contributions from refractive and diffractive effects created by ionospheric irregularities. The modeling will incorporate data sets from other geospace sensors  including incoherent and coherent scatter radar systems  to help constrain and validate the modelling work and interpret the hf receiver data. Ionospheric scintillation signatures caused by refraction and diffraction will be identified and correlated with the occurrence of geophysical phenomena in the region  such as large scale plasma density irregularities and particle precipitation  as well as regional geomagnetic conditions to identify scintillation generation mechanisms and sources. The hf receiver measurements at the heart of this work are unique in that provide signal amplitude  phase  and polarization parameters of the received signal in the polar cap at a sub-second temporal resolution - a capability that is virtually unprecedented in polar-cap radio science. As such  the proposed work has the potential to dramatically transform our knowledge of ionospheric scintillation and plasma dynamics in the region by providing the necessary data and analysis that can be used to advance modeling and other theoretical work targeting the complex plasma processes generating plasma density irregularities and scintillation in the region. The proposed work is timely and directly responds to the heliophysics lws solicitation. It will undertake ''modeling and validation of ionospheric irregularities and scintillation'' (FST # 1). Its objectives and science questions address each of the combined objectives of the 2014 science plan and the 2013 national research council decadal strategy for solar and space physics report  outlined on page b.1-1 of the NASA roses 2020 document. The work will provide the critical information required to study and characterize the ionospheric scintillation in the space environment advance the understanding of the connection between these phenomena and its space weather drivers and produce results from which scintillation detection and forecasting methodologies can be developed to mitigate their negative effects.

recipient_location.address_line1: 323 DR MARTIN LUTHER KING JR BLVD
